agent_output:
```json
{
  "summary": {
    "verdict": "BLOCK",
    "one_liner": "Prototype pollution via unsanitized user input passed directly to deepMerge"
  },

  "critical_findings": [
    {
      "title": "Prototype Pollution via __proto__ / constructor",
      "risk": "Attacker sends `{\"__proto__\": {\"isAdmin\": true}}` as request body, polluting Object.prototype globally. Every object in the process inherits `isAdmin: true`, enabling privilege escalation, auth bypass, or RCE depending on downstream code.",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "deepMerge() — `for (const key in source)` iterates over all enumerable properties including `__proto__` and `constructor`; no key sanitization exists",
      "reproduction": "curl -X POST /delivery-preferences -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{\"__proto__\": {\"isAdmin\": true}}' — then in any subsequent handler: `({}).isAdmin === true`",
      "fix": "Guard the loop: skip keys `__proto__`, `constructor`, `prototype`. Add `if (!Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(source, key)) continue;` and `if (key === '__proto__' || key === 'constructor' || key === 'prototype') continue;`. Better yet, validate `userPreferences` against an allow-list schema of known preference keys before merging.",
      "time_to_fix": "15 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Security: no untrusted input should modify global object state"
    },
    {
      "title": "No input validation — arbitrary keys persisted to database",
      "risk": "Without schema validation, any JSON keys from the request body are merged into defaults and written to the database. An attacker can inject arbitrary fields (e.g., `{\"role\": \"admin\", \"creditBalance\": 99999}`) that may be trusted by other services reading the same record.",
      "severity": "High",
      "location": "applyDeliveryPreferences() — `req.body` is passed directly to `deepMerge` with no validation",
      "reproduction": "curl -X POST /delivery-preferences -d '{\"internalFlag\": true, \"discount\": 100}' — these fields are persisted verbatim",
      "fix": "Destructure or pick only known keys: `const { leaveAtDoor, requireSignature, notifyBySms } = req.body;` and merge only those. Alternatively, use a schema validator (zod, joi) to reject unknown keys.",
      "time_to_fix": "20 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Data integrity: only valid delivery preference fields should be stored"
    },
    {
      "title": "deepMerge treats arrays as plain objects, corrupting data",
      "risk": "If a preference value is an array (e.g., `preferredTimeSlots: [\"morning\"]`), the `typeof === 'object'` check is true and the code does `target[key] = {}`, destroying the array and merging numeric indices into a plain object. Data is silently corrupted.",
      "severity": "High",
      "location": "deepMerge() — `if (source[key] && typeof source[key] === 'object')` matches arrays; `target[key] = {}` coerces them to objects",
      "reproduction": "Send `{\"preferredTimeSlots\": [\"morning\", \"evening\"]}` — stored as `{\"0\": \"morning\", \"1\": \"evening\"}` instead of an array",
      "fix": "Add an Array.isArray check: `if (Array.isArray(source[key])) { target[key] = [...source[key]]; } else if (...)`. Or replace the custom deepMerge entirely with a battle-tested library.",
      "time_to_fix": "10 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Correctness: merged preferences must preserve data types"
    }
  ],

  "important_issues": [
    {
      "title": "deepMerge mutates the defaults object",
      "description": "The `defaults` object is defined as a `const` local, so it's re-created per call — not a module-level singleton. This is safe today, but if anyone hoists `defaults` to module scope for reuse (a common refactor), every request will mutate the shared defaults, causing cross-request data leakage.",
      "location": "applyDeliveryPreferences() — `deepMerge(defaults, userPreferences)` mutates `defaults` in place"
    },
    {
      "title": "No error handling on DB write or input parsing",
      "description": "If `db.deliveries.updatePreferences` throws (connection failure, constraint violation), the error is unhandled and will crash the request or leak a stack trace. No try/catch or error middleware is visible.",
      "location": "applyDeliveryPreferences() — `await db.deliveries.updatePreferences(...)` with no error handling"
    }
  ],

  "suggestions": [
    "Replace the hand-rolled deepMerge with a well-tested utility (lodash.merge with prototype-pollution guards, or structured-clone + Object.assign for shallow needs)",
    "Add request body size limits to prevent large payload DoS against the merge function",
    "Return the merged preferences in the response so the client can confirm what was saved"
  ],

  "production_readiness": {
    "deployment_risks": "The prototype pollution vulnerability is exploitable immediately upon deployment by any authenticated user. A single malicious request can compromise the entire Node.js process for all users.",
    "rollback_capability": "Rollback of the code is straightforward, but polluted Object.prototype persists in-memory until process restart. Corrupted database records from arbitrary-key injection require manual cleanup.",
    "backward_compatibility": "N/A — new endpoint, no backward compatibility concerns",
    "monitoring_coverage": "No logging or monitoring on what keys are being written. Malicious payloads will be invisible without request-level audit logging.",
    "breaking_changes": {
      "api_breaking": false,
      "db_destructive": false,
      "shared_library": false,
      "auth_security": true,
      "data_pipeline": false,
      "config_changes": false,
      "details": ["Prototype pollution can bypass authentication/authorization checks globally across the process"]
    },
    "safe_to_deploy": false,
    "rollback_plan": "Revert deployment and restart all Node.js processes to clear polluted prototypes. Audit delivery_preferences table for injected keys and clean affected rows."
  },

  "escalations": [],

  "confidence": {
    "level": 97,
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "Prototype pollution is a well-documented, mechanically reproducible vulnerability — the code has zero guards",
      "Missing input validation is directly observable from the code",
      "Array-as-object bug is deterministic given typeof [] === 'object'"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Unknown whether upstream middleware (e.g., helmet, express body-parser config) provides any partial mitigation for prototype pollution"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "req.body is parsed JSON from an untrusted client with no upstream schema validation",
      "No global prototype-pollution middleware (like express-json-safeguard) is applied elsewhere"
    ]
  }
}
```

content.thinking:
Let me analyze this code for security and correctness issues.

Key hypotheses:
1. Prototype pollution via `__proto__`, `constructor`, `prototype` keys in user input
2. No input validation - arbitrary keys injected into DB
3. Array handling broken in deepMerge
